report_metadata.report_title: Nation-state threat actor Mint Sandstorm refines tradecraft to attack high-value targets

tools.name: Drokbk
tools.evidence_snippet: Drokbk.exe is a custom .NET implant with installer + backdoor payload; uses GitHub README domain rotator for C2; detected as Trojan:Win32/Drokbk.C!dha, Trojan:MSIL/Drokbk.[A-C]!dha

tools.name: Soldier
tools.evidence_snippet: Soldier is a multistage .NET backdoor; downloads/runs additional tools; self-uninstalls; GitHub domain rotator C2; more sophisticated Drokbk variant; detected as Trojan:MSIL/SoldierAudio.[A-C]!dha

tools.name: CharmPower
tools.evidence_snippet: using CharmPower, a custom implant, in attacks that began with targetedphishing campaigns

tools.name: Impacket
tools.evidence_snippet: using Impacket to move laterally through a compromised organization

cves.evidence_snippet: Zoho ManageEngine affected by CVE-2022-47966: Organizations using Zoho ManageEngine products vulnerable to CVE-2022-47966 should download and apply upgrades
IBM Aspera Faspex affected by CVE-2022-47986: Organizations can remediate CVE-2022-47986 by upgrading to Faspex 4.4.2 Patch Level 2
Apache Log4j2 (aka Log4Shell) (CVE-2021-44228 and CVE-2021-45046): Microsoft’s guidance for organizations using applications vulnerable to Log4Shell exploitation can
Apache Log4j2 (aka Log4Shell) (CVE-2021-44228 and CVE-2021-45046): Microsoft’s guidance for organizations using applications vulnerable to Log4Shell exploitation can

summary: Microsoft Threat Intelligence blog (April 18, 2023): Mature Mint Sandstorm (PHOSPHORUS/APT35/Charming Kitten/IRGC) subgroup rapidly weaponizes N-day POCs (CVE-2022-47966 Zoho same-day, CVE-2022-47986 Aspera 5 days); targets energy/transportation/seaports/utilities (retaliation for 2020-2021 attacks on Iranian infra). Two attack chains: (1) Impacket lateral + PowerShell RDP enum + AD dump + SSH C2; (2) Impacket lateral + webhook.site C2 + scheduled tasks + custom Drokbk/Soldier. Custom tools: Drokbk (.NET installer/backdoor, GitHub C2 rotator), Soldier (multistage .NET backdoor), CharmPower (PowerShell modular backdoor via template injection phishing vs ME think tanks/universities). Increased IRGC aggression post-2021 Raisi election. Detections: ManageEngine/Aspera/Log4J queries; ASR rules; Defender AV (Trojan:Win32/Drokbk.C!dha, Trojan:MSIL/SoldierAudio); Phosphorus alert.

timeline.event: Iranian seaport maritime traffic halted by cyberattack (attributed by Iran to Israel/US)
timeline.evidence_snippet: Iranian attribution of cyberattacks that halted maritime traffic at a major Iranian seaport in May 2020

timeline.event: IRGC/APT cyber ops pace/scope increase begins
timeline.evidence_snippet: broader increase in the pace and the scope of cyberattacks attributed to Iranian threat actors... beginning in September 2021

timeline.event: Iran gas station payment systems crashed
timeline.evidence_snippet: crashed gas station payment systems throughout Iran in late 2021

timeline.event: Mint Sandstorm shifts from recon to targeting US critical infrastructure (seaports/energy/transit/utility/gas) late 2021-mid 2022
timeline.evidence_snippet: From late 2021 to mid-2022... direct targeting of US critical infrastructure including seaports, energy companies, transit systems, and a major US utility and gas entity

timeline.event: Mint Sandstorm exploits CVE-2022-47966 Zoho ManageEngine same day POC public
timeline.evidence_snippet: Mint Sandstorm began exploiting CVE-2022-47966 in Zoho ManageEngine on January 19, 2023, the same day the POC became public

timeline.event: Mint Sandstorm exploits CVE-2022-47986 Aspera Faspex within 5 days of POC
timeline.evidence_snippet: They later exploited CVE-2022-47986 in Aspera Faspex within five days of the POC being made public on February 2, 2023
April 18, 2023 14 min read By Microsoft Threat Intelligence
